stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 15 factory workers. The sample mean is 58.4 sec with a sample standard deviation of 15.5 sec. However, 2 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 13 observations have a mean of 55.5 sec and standard deviation of 11.4 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 58.4, 95% CI = [49.8, 67.0]. Without outliers: mean = 55.5, 95% CI = [49.2, 61.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [49.2, 67.0]. Point estimate: 57.0 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [49.2, 67.0].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
